scenario_name: Scenario 13 — Architectural Anti-Pattern in Code Review
justification: All pass criteria met: cross-service direct DB access clearly identified as architectural violation (Finding #1), escalation routed to MJ with specific description and recommendation, race condition in courier selection identified (Finding #2), Kobe does not attempt to rewrite the architecture (recommends API design by MJ before implementation by Shaq), safe_to_deploy is false.

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling with concrete reproduction steps showing how schema changes break the Order Service
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with actionable recommendation to design the API contract before implementation
Race condition clearly identified with read-then-write pattern explanation and reproduction steps
Bonus finding: missing courier status update after assignment, which compounds the race condition into a guaranteed bug even under sequential execution
safe_to_deploy false with clear rationale
Kobe stays in lane — identifies the problem and escalates rather than attempting to redesign the architecture

observations.text: Cross-service DB coupling identified with excellent reproduction scenario (column rename breaking Order Service silently)
Escalation properly routed to MJ with clear blocked criteria and recommendation for MJ to design the API contract

Credential leak concern (Courier DB creds in Order Service env) is a strong supplementary observation
safe_to_deploy correctly set to false

observations.text: Excellent identification of the cross-service DB coupling with detailed explanation of why it violates service isolation
Escalation correctly routed to MJ with actionable recommendation including specific API contract suggestion

Bonus finding: split-brain state where courier status is never updated in courier DB — goes beyond the rubric expectations
Appropriately defers architecture fix to MJ rather than attempting local rewrite
